architecture_summary: Perceiver is a Transformer-based architecture that uses asymmetric cross-attention to project very large inputs into a fixed-size latent bottleneck, then processes the latents with a deep Transformer stack. It iteratively alternates cross-attention and latent self-attention, optionally sharing weights across repeats, and uses position/modality encodings such as Fourier features to handle images, audio, video, and point clouds without domain-specific convolutions.

novel_contributions: a Transformer-based model that scales to more than a hundred thousand inputs
an asymmetric attention mechanism that iteratively distills inputs into a tight latent bottleneck
a single architecture that handles images, point clouds, audio, video, and multimodal combinations without domain-specific assumptions
Fourier feature position encodings adapted for scalable multimodal inputs
iterative cross-attention with optional weight sharing to improve parameter efficiency

key_claims.claim: The Perceiver scales to hundreds of thousands of inputs by using an asymmetric attention mechanism and a latent bottleneck, while remaining flexible across modalities.
The Perceiver obtains performance comparable to ResNet-50 and ViT on ImageNet without 2D convolutions by directly attending to 50,000 pixels.
The architecture is competitive in all modalities in AudioSet.
On ImageNet, the Perceiver is competitive with models specifically designed for processing images and reaches 78.0 top-1 validation accuracy in Table 1.
On AudioSet, the Perceiver achieves near state-of-the-art results on audio-only, video-only, and audio+video settings, with 44.2 mAP for the tuned mel spectrogram model.
On ModelNet40, the Perceiver reaches 85.7 accuracy and outperforms the generic baselines listed in Table 4, though it remains below PointNet++.
Weight sharing reduces parameters by approximately 10x and improves validation performance on ImageNet.
